method: Full training pipeline audit + figure generation audit + runtime E2E test

architecture.key_finding: All 11 architectural sub-claims verified. HeteroGAT, single layer, 6-dim, 128 hidden, 4 heads.

datasets.key_finding: All 6 public datasets present. AMI 0.717 max confirmed (TON_IoT). Real SOC deployment infrastructure exists but no deployment data.

key_findings: NTXentLoss is dead code - never used in canonical training pipeline
multidomain_v2 trained with SimCLR + supervised CrossEntropyLoss on campaign labels
network_v9_v3 trained with custom topological_ntxent_loss (67%) + SimCLR (33%)
train_hybrid_v10.py (only NT-Xent user) marked as FAILED by authors
Zero-shot works on only 2/5 datasets (NSL-KDD 0.743, UNSW-NB15 0.538)
Topological = co-occurrence edges, not mathematical topology

explainability.key_finding: HGNNExplainer, AttentionExtractor, PCA/UMAP all functional. SHAP needs manual install.

key_findings: All 15 ablation CSV files contain identical placeholder values
generate_figures.py uses hardcoded values for all 10 figures
aggregate_results.py computes statistics on placeholder data
Hardcoded figure values exceed all real experimental results
Zero-shot beats per-dataset training is false on 3/5 datasets
Systematic fabrication of experimental validation section

summary: Architecture and explainability claims are accurate (~95%). Dataset claims mostly accurate (~90%). Self-supervised/zero-shot narrative is misleading (35%) - NT-Xent is dead code, canonical checkpoint uses supervised labels. Experimental validation is systematically fabricated (19%) - all ablation CSVs are placeholders, all figures use hardcoded values.
